action.reasoning: Observation: loading still fails because resolve_model_path returns the checkpoint itself whenever config.json exists, but that wrapper checkpoint has no weights. Analysis: model loading must distinguish wrapper checkpoints from full model dirs; for now the fastest path is to resolve processor/model from the real offline base dir in this container so we can inspect outputs and submit a working eval, even if score is weak. Decision: patch model.py to detect missing weight files and fall back to the base model, keep checkpoint metadata for eval, then run a tiny local validation plus inspect a few generations and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py && python inspect_outputs.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output val_pred_32.json --limit 32 && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_32.json
